title: deletedAt: null triggers soft-delete instead of undelete
description: In message_set update, path=='deletedAt' with value is_null() calls soft_delete_message(msg_id, false). Trigger: PATCH {"deletedAt": null}. Bad behavior: the message is soft-deleted, but null semantically means 'not deleted' / clear the field. An API user expecting null to un-delete the message gets the opposite. Pre-existing, not introduced by this diff.
close_reason: Fixed: deletedAt now requires a string; null is rejected with invalidArguments instead of silently soft-deleting
